summary.intent: Run the local validation suite (`./scripts/local-validate.sh`) to verify the codebase passes all checks before committing or pushing.
summary.outcome: Two failures were fixed: rustfmt formatting drift in two Rust files was corrected, and `scripts/check-file-size.sh` was patched to be compatible with macOS bash 3.2 (which lacks `declare -A`).

repo: local-validate.sh invokes check commands via `bash -lc "$cmd"`, which resolves to /bin/bash (3.2) on macOS in a login shell context, not the Homebrew bash 4+ that may be in PATH during normal sessions.
The project uses cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 for rustfmt formatting checks.

The project runs on macOS without CUDA, so local validation skips --all-features; override via LOCAL_VALIDATE_LINT_CMD / LOCAL_VALIDATE_TEST_CMD / LOCAL_VALIDATE_BUILD_CMD / LOCAL_VALIDATE_COVERAGE_CMD.
llama-cpp-sys-2 cached build dirs are cleaned at the start of each local validation run.

code.finding: Used `declare -A` (associative array, bash 4+ only), which fails under macOS /bin/bash 3.2. Replaced with a loop-based approach for bash 3.2 compatibility.
Rustfmt reformatted inline ternary-style `if` expressions in arithmetic into multi-line blocks.
Vault unseal test module had scattered `use` statements that rustfmt consolidated into grouped `use {}` blocks; many trait impl methods were missing blank lines between them that rustfmt added.

workflow: On macOS, scripts that use bash associative arrays (`declare -A`) must either explicitly invoke bash 4+ or use bash 3.2-compatible alternatives, because `bash -lc` in a login shell resolves to /bin/bash 3.2.
Running `cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt --all` fixes formatting drift before re-running validation.

friction: `local-validate.sh` uses `bash -lc` to run sub-checks, silently switching to /bin/bash 3.2 on macOS, causing `declare -A` failures that are non-obvious to diagnose.
Rustfmt formatting drift accumulated across two files and required a separate fix pass before the suite could pass.
open_items: Commit the two fixes (rustfmt changes + check-file-size.sh bash 3.2 patch) — user was asked but no confirmation shown in transcript.
Consider adding a shebang or explicit bash version guard in check-file-size.sh to surface the bash version requirement clearly for future contributors.
